headlines.description: — Donovan Mitchell scored a season-high 46 points, Jarrett Allen had 24 points and 10 rebounds and the Cleveland Cavaliers pulled away in the second half for a 132-121 victory over the Philadelphia 76ers on Tuesday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Mitchell's season-high 46 points lead Cavaliers to 132-121 victory over 76ers

headlines.description: — Cade Cunningham scored 19 of his 31 points in the fourth quarter as the Detroit Pistons won their fourth straight, beating the Utah Jazz 114-103 on Wednesday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cade Cunningham scores 31 points, leads Pistons past Jazz 114-103

headlines.description: — Michael Porter Jr. had 32 points and 11 rebounds, and the Brooklyn Nets overcame the loss of leading scorer Cam Thomas to claim their first victory of the season in a 112-103 road win over the Indiana Pacers on Wednesday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Porter scores 32 as Nets beat Pacers 112-103 for 1st win

headlines.description: — Jaylen Brown scored 35 points and the Boston Celtics shook off a double-digit first-quarter deficit to beat Washington 136-107 on Wednesday night and send the Wizards to their sixth straight loss.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brown scores 35 points in 26 minutes before watching Celtics coast to 136-107 win over Wizards

events.name: Minnesota Timberwolves at New York Knicks

headlines.description: — OG Anunoby scored 25 points, Jalen Brunson had 23 points, 10 assists and seven rebounds, and the New York Knicks spoiled Anthony Edwards' return to the Minnesota Timberwolves' lineup with a 137-114 victory on Wednesday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Anunoby and Brunson help Knicks rout Timberwolves 137-114 in Anthony Edwards' return from injury

headlines.description: — Russell Westbrook posted his NBA-record 204th career triple-double and Dennis Schroder sparked a key fourth-quarter spurt that gave the Sacramento Kings a 121-116 win over the Golden State Warriors on Wednesday night in a matchup of short-handed teams.
headlines.shortLinkText: Westbrook's triple-double and Schroder's clutch run lift Kings over Warriors 121-116
